draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer a conversational, approachable tone over corporate formality
facade.label: Casual and chatty
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into personality, humor, and natural language in copy and interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Favor polished, authoritative, business-focused voice and minimal personality

evidence.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer a conversational, approachable tone over corporate formality
evidence.implication: Lean into personality, humor, and natural language in copy and interactions

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer organic, approachable interfaces over sharp geometric precision
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Favor warm, humanized design with curved edges, softer shadows, and gentle micro-interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Favor clean, sharp, structured design with hard lines, high contrast, and minimal ornamentation

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers low-light, high-contrast workspaces that reduce eye strain during long focus sessions
facade.label: Dark mode interface
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark backgrounds, bright text, and minimal glare — likely values comfort and reduced cognitive load
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer bright, airy interfaces or neutral tones — test light-based palettes next

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate visual feedback over animated transitions in an AI workspace where rapid thought-capture matters
facade.axisTargeted: Latency vs. smoothness—do interactions feel snappy-and-direct or fluid-and-reassuring?
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: AI workspace prioritizes responsiveness and directness; animations may feel like friction; user wants to feel in control
facade.rejectImplies: Smooth transitions and motion build confidence; users want to see their action taken seriously; instant can feel jarring or incomplete

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/supportive tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs snappy/instant interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/supportive tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs snappy/instant interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer scannable dashboards with key metrics in cards over dense tables or narrative explanations
facade.axisTargeted: Information density and visual hierarchy — do they favor quick-scan summaries or comprehensive detail?
facade.label: Dashboard at a glance
facade.acceptImplies: They want immediate overview; likely prefer KPI cards, widgets, status indicators, and visual hierarchy over scrolling through dense data
facade.rejectImplies: They may prefer immersive data views, detailed tables, or narrative context; less interested in abstracted summaries or segmented card layouts

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer organizing tools and contexts in a persistent left sidebar, with secondary navigation as tabs within each section, rather than top-level tab bars or card-based layouts.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation architecture—sidebar vs. top tabs vs. floating panels
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs inside
facade.acceptImplies: Explore vertical information hierarchies, collapsible sections in sidebars, and tab-based micro-navigation. Test whether users want persistent context on the left and fluid content on the right.
facade.rejectImplies: Investigate horizontal tab systems at the top, card-based navigation, or full-screen modal switches. The user may prefer less persistent scaffolding or flatter organization.

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace may prefer a professional, corporate tone that signals enterprise-grade capability and trustworthiness
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality (friendly-casual vs professional-formal spectrum)
facade.label: All business
facade.acceptImplies: Design language should emphasize competence, structure, and authority; use formal terminology and polished, minimal aesthetics
facade.rejectImplies: Professional tone is not the primary driver; conversational warmth and accessibility matter more than signaling corporate legitimacy

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
